content.problemStatement:
Recently the company Life Ltd created a new logo for themselves. You are asked to test the design of the logo.

The logo is a 3×33 \times 33×3 square grid with 9 cells. Each cell contains some lowercase English letter. This logo will be considered good if there exist three cells in the shape of an L that contain the letter 'l' (lowercase 'L') in each of them. That is, there should be a cell with 'l', its cell directly beneath it should also have 'l', and the cell to the right of the second cell should also have 'l'.

Your task is to tell whether the logo is good or not.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains an integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of the test cases follows.
Each of the next three lines contains a description of the logo, i-th of the line contains three characters which denote the i-th row of the logo.


Output Format
For each test case, output `yes` or `no` according to the answer to the problem.
constraints: Each character in the logo is a lowercase English letter
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of three lines, each containing three characters representing the logo.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output 'yes' if the logo is good, otherwise output 'no'.

sampleTestCases.input:
3
laz
lla
aaa
ala
lla
aaa
lll
lll
lll
sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the L-shaped cells with 'l' are in the first and second rows. For the second test case, there are no L-shaped cells with 'l'. For the third test case, all cells contain 'l', so it is also good.

solution.approach: To determine if the logo is good, we need to check all possible L-shaped combinations of cells for the presence of 'l'. We can do this by iterating over each cell in the first two rows and checking if the cell below it and the cell to its right (in the case of the first row) or below it (in the case of the second row) contain 'l'. If any such combination is found, the logo is considered good.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) — since for each test case, we perform a constant amount of work to check all possible L-shaped combinations.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — as we only use a constant amount of space to store the input and the result for each test case.
